methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Oklo
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Nano Nuclear Energy is a public-stage nuclear energy company currently valued at $1.5B+, having raised over $600M to date. The comparable universe consists of 11 total peers across the nuclear energy sector—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms—with a focus set of 5 peers spanning late-stage private and early public stages: TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Kairos Power ($1.6B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B).

A peer-median valuation methodology was applied across four same-sector comparables in the similar valuation tier, yielding a median of $1.8B. This approach reflects the median positioning within a peer range of $1.0B to $4.0B, anchored by Zap Energy at the lower bound and TerraPower at the upper bound. The peer set captures heterogeneous development stages, from Series D through late-stage private and public listings, which introduces some dispersion but remains appropriate given Nano Nuclear's hybrid positioning between private and public markets.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.5B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.8B. The current valuation of $1.5B sits at the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking timelines, regulatory pathway clarity for advanced reactors, and capital intensity requirements through commercialization—factors that drive wide peer valuation dispersion and limit the strength of comparable company inference at this stage.
